title: First Chinese Illustrator Wins Andersen Award
source: The Guardian - Beijing Review - Vol. 69 No. 17, 23 April 2026
content:
Cai Gao recently made history by becoming the first Chinese illustrator to win the prestigious Hans Christian Andersen Award. Recognized as the highest international honor for children's literature, the announcement was made at the Bologna Children's Book Fair in Italy. Born in Changsha, Hunan Province, in 1946, Cai has dedicated her life to publishing and creating children's books since the 1970s. She began her inspiring career as a rural primary school teacher before transitioning into a role as an editor. Jury President Shereen Kreidieh praised her works for demonstrating exceptional quality, artistry, and emotional engagement.

Upon receiving the award, Cai humbly stated that the honor felt too significant for her to carry alone. She emphasized that the award represents the hard work of an entire generation of illustrators and creators. Furthermore, she shared the accolade with her colleagues in the publishing industry who possess a deep love for picture books. Together, they have witnessed and contributed to the birth and vibrant development of Chinese children's literature. Her achievement marks a monumental milestone for picture books in China, inspiring future artists to continue this creative legacy.
